summary: Decent recording that captures a brutal, top‑ten "Other One" and a shining "Wharf Rat", though the era’s keyboard transition divides opinion.
review: The three fans agree the tape is listenable – “decent capture” and “nice recording” – and they all single out the ferocious performance of The Other One as a highlight, with Wharf Rat also getting praise. Keyboard work sparks mixed feelings (the Brent‑to‑Vince/Bruce shift), but overall the show is remembered fondly despite no explicit source details.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical moments (especially The Other One) push the rating up, while only modest comments on recording quality keep it from higher marks; three consistent reviews give moderate confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the capture as decent and nice, with no major drop‑outs or muddiness noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: No detailed set‑list commentary, but fans highlight the high energy of The Other One and a solid performance overall; keyboard transition is noted as a point of personal taste.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His playing on The Other One is praised, especially how Hornsby’s piano complemented Garcia’s lead.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned for dropping “bombs” during transitions that added intensity to the jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mixed reactions to the shift from Brent Mydland to Vince/Wm. Bruce; some miss Brent’s style while others appreciate Hornsby’s piano contributions.
